cases.title: First registration for participant/event pair creates exactly one active row
preconditions: Event is RegistrationOpen with available seats and active registration window
Participant has no existing registration for the event
steps: Invoke registrationService.register(eventId, participantId, actorContext)
Query registrations for (eventId, participantId) where state is active (Requested, Registered, Waitlisted, CheckedIn)
cases.expected: Registration succeeds with a single active row; INV-01 and BR-01 satisfied (at most one active registration per participant per event)

cases.title: Second registration attempt while Registered is rejected
preconditions: Event RegistrationOpen with available seats
Participant already has active Registered registration for the event
steps: Invoke registrationService.register(eventId, participantId, actorContext) for the same participant a second time
Observe error and count active registration rows
cases.expected: Rejected with ApiError code REGISTRATION_DUPLICATE_ACTIVE and HTTP 409; no second active row created; existing Registered state unchanged

cases.title: BR-01 maps to REGISTRATION_DUPLICATE_ACTIVE error code
preconditions: Domain rule catalog loaded per validation rules doc
steps: Resolve RULE_TO_ERROR_CODE for business rule BR-01
Assert BUSINESS_RULE_IDS.BR_01 equals BR-01
cases.expected: BR-01 resolves to VALIDATION_ERROR_CODES.REGISTRATION_DUPLICATE_ACTIVE; mapping is stable and deterministic

cases.title: assertNoDuplicateActive rejects existing active registration row
preconditions: Existing registration row in an active state (Registered, Waitlisted, Requested, or CheckedIn)
steps: Call assertNoDuplicateActive(existingRegistrationRow)
cases.expected: Throws ApiError with code REGISTRATION_DUPLICATE_ACTIVE and statusCode 409; details include existing registrationId and state

cases.title: Duplicate POST registrations returns 409 with typed error envelope
preconditions: Published event with registration opened and available capacity
Participant JWT used to successfully POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations once
Fresh Idempotency-Key header for duplicate attempt
steps: POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations with the same participant Authorization bearer a second time
Parse error response and GET registration-status
cases.expected: HTTP 409 with error.code REGISTRATION_DUPLICATE_ACTIVE and user-safe message; registration-status still returns the original single active registration unchanged

cases.title: Participant UI prevents duplicate registration with clear feedback
preconditions: Participant logged in with active Registered status for an open event
Event detail page displays current registration state
steps: Navigate to event detail for the event where participant is already registered
Attempt to register again via UI (register button, retry, or equivalent)
Observe inline or toast error messaging
cases.expected: UI does not create a second registration; participant sees duplicate-registration feedback consistent with REGISTRATION_DUPLICATE_ACTIVE server semantics

cases.title: Duplicate blocked when participant is already Waitlisted
preconditions: Event is full with waitlistEnabled true
Participant already has active Waitlisted registration for the event
steps: Attempt registrationService.register for the same participant and event again
cases.expected: Rejected with REGISTRATION_DUPLICATE_ACTIVE (409); single Waitlisted row and waitlistPosition unchanged; no second waitlist entry

cases.title: Duplicate blocked when participant is already CheckedIn
preconditions: Participant has active registration in CheckedIn state for the event
steps: Attempt registrationService.register for the same participant and event
cases.expected: Rejected with REGISTRATION_DUPLICATE_ACTIVE; CheckedIn registration remains the sole active record

cases.title: Duplicate blocked when registration is still in Requested state
preconditions: Participant has registration row in Requested state (in-flight, not yet accepted or rejected)
steps: Attempt a second registrationService.register for the same participant and event before first request completes
cases.expected: Second attempt rejected with REGISTRATION_DUPLICATE_ACTIVE; Requested row remains the only active registration

cases.title: Re-registration permitted after cancellation when no active row remains
preconditions: Participant previously Registered then cancelled (CancelledByUser) before deadline
Event RegistrationOpen with available seats
No active registration exists for participant on this event
steps: Invoke registrationService.register for the same participant after cancellation completed
Query active registrations for participant/event pair
cases.expected: New registration succeeds with state Registered; BR-01 satisfied because cancelled state is not active; not treated as duplicate

cases.title: Concurrent duplicate registration requests yield single active row
preconditions: Event RegistrationOpen with available seats
Same participantId used in parallel registrationService.register calls with no prior registration
steps: Fire two or more concurrent register calls for the same participantId and eventId
Count active registration rows for the participant/event pair
cases.expected: Exactly one request succeeds; all other concurrent attempts rejected with REGISTRATION_DUPLICATE_ACTIVE or equivalent conflict; database never holds more than one active registration per participant/event

cases.title: Duplicate POST with new Idempotency-Key still rejected
preconditions: Participant successfully registered via POST with Idempotency-Key K1
Second POST prepared with different Idempotency-Key K2
steps: POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations with Idempotency-Key K2 for the same authenticated participant
cases.expected: HTTP 409 with REGISTRATION_DUPLICATE_ACTIVE; BR-01 dedupe guard applies independently of idempotency key reuse

cases.title: assertNoDuplicateActive permits registration when prior row is terminal inactive
preconditions: Existing registration row in terminal inactive state (Rejected, CancelledByUser, CancelledByOrganizer, Expired, Absent, or Attended)
steps: Call assertNoDuplicateActive(terminalInactiveRegistrationRow)
cases.expected: Function completes without throwing; registration pipeline may proceed because terminal states are not active per INV-01

cases.title: Dedupe validation runs before capacity assignment in registration pipeline
preconditions: Event full with waitlist disabled (would otherwise reject with REGISTRATION_REJECTED_FULL)
Participant already has active Registered registration
steps: Attempt second registration for same participant
Inspect error code on failure
cases.expected: Failure code is REGISTRATION_DUPLICATE_ACTIVE (not REGISTRATION_REJECTED_FULL), confirming pipeline step 3 (dedup active registration) executes before step 4 (capacity decision)

cases.title: P0 invariant: persistence never holds more than one active registration per participant/event
preconditions: Database with partial unique index uq_active_registration on (event_id, participant_id) for active states
Any sequence of registration attempts including retries and concurrent calls
steps: After exercising duplicate-block scenarios, query registrations for participantId and eventId where state IN (Requested, Registered, Waitlisted, CheckedIn)
cases.expected: Active row count is never greater than 1; aligns with testing plan P0 severity for duplicate active registration defects

cases.title: BR-01 rule evaluation is deterministic for identical inputs
preconditions: Fixed participantId, eventId, and existing active registration fixture
steps: Evaluate BR-01 dedupe guard twice with identical inputs
Compare error code, message, and details
cases.expected: Both evaluations produce identical REGISTRATION_DUPLICATE_ACTIVE outcome; same inputs always yield same output per determinism requirements
